topic: Sourcegraph Cody Review 2026: AI Code Assistant for Large Codebases

competitors.title: Cody AI Review (Sourcegraph): AI Code Assistant (2026) - BuildPilot
key_points: Cody's core advantage is deep codebase context — it indexes entire repos via Sourcegraph, unlike Copilot (limited to open files) or Cursor (good context but not full-repo)
Supports multiple LLM backends: Claude Sonnet/Opus, GPT-4o, Gemini, Mixtral — switch models mid-conversation based on task
Commands system with /explain, /edit, /test, /doc, /smell plus custom commands for project-specific workflows
Available across VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, web, CLI — broader IDE support than Cursor
Autocomplete is slower than Copilot/Cursor due to context retrieval latency — trades speed for depth
Setup complexity higher than Copilot or Cursor — enterprise requires Sourcegraph instance
Pricing: Free (500 autocompletes/mo, 20 chat/day), Pro $9/mo (unlimited), Enterprise $19/user/month (full codebase)
Rating: 7.5/10 — best for enterprise developers in large codebases, not ideal for small projects or autocomplete speed

competitors.title: Sourcegraph Cody Pricing 2026: Free–$19/user/month Compared - CostBench
key_points: Pricing tiers: Free (free, 200 chat messages/month, unlimited autocomplete), Pro $9/user/month (unlimited chat, extended context), Enterprise $19/user/month (full codebase context)
Budget-friendly positioning vs competitors: Copilot $0-39/mo, Cursor $0-40/mo, Codeium $0-15/mo, Aider Free-$100/mo
1 documented hidden cost: account termination without refund (Trustpilot complaint)
Free tier is more generous than Copilot's — unlimited autocomplete even on free plan
Pro at $9/month is among the cheapest unlimited AI coding plans
Enterprise tier provides codebase-wide AI understanding, cross-repo context, admin controls, SSO/SAML
Year 1 total cost: $0 (free tier developer), $9 (Pro power user), $19 (Enterprise for large codebase)

competitors.title: Qodo vs Cody (Sourcegraph): AI Code Review Compared (2026) - DEV Community
key_points: Cody is built on Sourcegraph's code intelligence platform indexing billions of lines of code since 2013
Core differentiator is context: retrieves relevant code from entire repository or across all repos in organization
Cody vs Qodo: Cody is a collaborator (codebase-aware assistant), Qodo is a gatekeeper (automated PR review) — complementary not competitive
IDE support: VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Emacs — broader than Qodo (VS Code, JetBrains only)
BYOK model flexibility: Bring Your Own Key for Claude, GPT-4, Gemini
Code search included via Sourcegraph code search — unique vs other AI assistants
Enterprise features: self-hosted Sourcegraph, on-premise deployment, zero data retention on Enterprise plan
Gartner Magic Quadrant: Cody not listed as Visionary (Qodo earned this in 2025)

competitors.title: Cody by Sourcegraph Review 2026 — Honest Pricing, Pros & Cons - AIToolSwitch
key_points: Best for: Developers and teams working with large, complex codebases needing context-aware assistance
Not ideal for: Users wanting offline AI coding or those with small isolated projects
Setup time: 10-20 minutes (moderate to high ease of use)
Key features: context-aware code completion, code explanation/documentation, automated refactoring, smart search across repos, customizable with codebase context
Pros: Deep codebase understanding, excellent for learning/onboarding, significant productivity boost
Cons: Resource-intensive for large projects, free tier limitations on usage
Integrations: VS Code, JetBrains IDEs, Sublime Text, GitLab, GitHub, Bitbucket
Alternatives: Continue.dev (free/open-source), Aider (free + LLM API costs), AskCodi ($12/month)

angles: Codebase Context is King: Why Sourcegraph Cody's full-repo understanding beats surface-level AI assistants for complex projects

Enterprise-Grade AI Coding: Cody's self-hosted deployment, SSO/SAML, and cross-repo intelligence make it the compliance-friendly choice

The Autocomplete Trade-off: Cody trades speed for context depth — when slower suggestions are actually better for understanding legacy code
